evidence: ui/src/lib/stores/websocket.js defines a layered command wrapper stack (`sendTypeCommand` -> `sendActionCommand` -> domain helpers) and then dozens of exported one-line command functions that only forward string literals and params.

ui/src/lib/stores/websocket.js `sendCommand` multiplexes two distinct transports (`sendPoke(payload)` in URBIT mode vs raw `SESSION.send(JSON.stringify(data))` in websocket mode), so one abstraction carries divergent runtime semantics.

goseg/web/_app/immutable/chunks/1.0d44cf41.js, 10.35bd59a0.js, 11.9463674b.js, and 12.0b2f13c7.js are pass-through re-export stubs (`import default as t ...; export { t as component };`) adding indirection without policy/translation.

goseg/web/_app/immutable/chunks/index.cac2eef5.js and goseg/web/_app/immutable/entry/start.a9edaa26.js show dense wrapper-heavy runtime indirection chains from bundled output, obscuring domain boundaries in reviewed artifacts.
No migration markers or deprecated/TODO-style residue were found in the assigned files (`rg` for TODO/FIXME/deprecated/console debug patterns returned no matches).
`ui/src/routes/+layout.js` contains only a concise type annotation comment and `prerender` export, with no formulaic AI-style boilerplate.

Both `goseg/web/_app/immutable/chunks/clipboard.750479b4.js` and `goseg/web/_app/immutable/chunks/sigil-js.esm.0c91ed14.js` are heavily minified bundle artifacts with compressed identifiers, which makes intentional vs generated-style ceremony hard to audit and increases long-term debt opacity.
ai_generated_debt.issues_preventing_higher_score: Prebuilt minified chunk artifacts in tracked source reduce reviewability and provenance clarity, so latent AI-style or migration residue cannot be confidently ruled out over time.

findings.summary: Command API is a large pass-through facade with minimal behavioral leverage and heavy stringly-typed forwarding.
evidence: websocket.js declares helper wrappers (`sendTypeCommand`, `sendActionCommand`, `sendSystemCommand`, `sendSetupCommand`, `sendStartramCommand`, `sendUrbitCommand`, `sendDevCommand`) and then a long list of exports that each just pass literals and params.

Many exports differ only by action strings and shallow object construction (for example `toggleWifi`, `connectWifi`, `startramRegister`, `toggleBackups`, `submitReport`), indicating low-cohesion command surface growth.
Global state stores in data.js (`URBIT_MODE`, `connected`, `structure`) are manipulated from the same module, increasing API breadth in one place.
findings.suggestion: Replace one-function-per-command wrappers with a typed command registry (domain + action schema + payload validator) and generate thin call sites from that registry; keep only behavior-bearing functions in the store module.

findings.summary: Single `sendCommand` abstraction leaks two different transport contracts behind one interface.
evidence: In websocket.js, `sendCommand` branches on `URBIT_MODE`: one path calls `sendPoke(payload)`, the other generates ids/tokens and writes serialized JSON via websocket `SESSION.send`.
Because the mode flag comes from a writable store (`URBIT_MODE` in data.js), call sites cannot infer which transport semantics apply from the function contract.
vite.config.js injects mode flags (`GS_URBIT_MODE`) at build/runtime boundaries, so transport behavior is externally toggled without an explicit transport interface boundary in code.
findings.suggestion: Introduce explicit transport adapters (e.g., `UrbitTransport`, `WebSocketTransport`) implementing a shared interface with documented request/ack semantics, and inject the selected adapter once during initialization instead of branching in each command send.

findings.summary: Multiple immutable chunk files are pure re-export wrappers that add call/import depth without domain value.
evidence: Each file is effectively only an import+export alias (`export { t as component }` and sometimes a `universal` re-export), with no behavior, policy, or translation.
These wrappers create additional module hops in the reviewed dependency surface while not serving as stable hand-written interfaces.
findings.suggestion: Treat these immutable build outputs as generated artifacts outside abstraction review/fix loops (review the source Svelte/routes instead), or stop committing these files where possible to avoid evaluating compiler indirection as architecture.

findings.summary: Bundled runtime files contain deep wrapper/utility indirection that obscures actual ownership boundaries for dependency analysis.
evidence: index.cac2eef5.js contains large numbers of micro-wrappers/aliases (e.g., identity wrappers and adapter helpers) that mostly serve compilation/runtime internals.

entry/start.a9edaa26.js and app.e0ffcb44.js orchestrate routing/runtime behavior through minified aliases and indirection chains, making abstraction boundaries non-honest from a maintainer perspective.
Modals/Nav compiled chunks import broad runtime symbol sets from index.cac2eef5.js, reinforcing low-signal dependency edges at artifact level.
findings.suggestion: Shift architectural review scope to pre-build source modules and keep immutable bundles out of abstraction health scoring; if bundles must remain, isolate them in a non-scored generated zone and add review guards to prevent artifact-level abstraction findings from masking real source-level issues.
